title: pavonia brevibracteolata
intro: Pavonia brevibracteolata, also known as the small-fruited rosemallow or Mexican rosemallow, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico, where it has been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Pavonia brevibracteolata are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, anthraquinones, and saponins, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced immune function.
